title: Authors and Citation
text: Joel Findlay. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Findlay J (2026). readAOFM: Reads data AOFM's dataHub. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://joel23978.github.io/readAOFM/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {readAOFM: Reads data from the AOFM's dataHub},   author = {Joel Findlay},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://joel23978.github.io/readAOFM/}, }

title: readAOFM
text: readAOFM downloads workbook data Australian Office Financial Management (AOFM) DataHub returns cleaned R data frames lists.

title: readAOFM
text: can install development version GitHub :

title: readAOFM
text: main entrypoint read_aofm(). security: one tb, tib, tn, slf, summary, aggregate, ownership, retail, term.premium; optional want supported tables type: one dealt, settlement, issuance, syndication, buyback, turnover; optional security return matching supported tables csv: TRUE, also writes parsed output CSV via underlying reader security omitted, read_aofm() returns named list supported tables. type omitted, returns supported tables security.

title: readAOFM
code: library(readAOFM)  tb_issuance <- read_aofm("tb", "issuance") head(tb_issuance)  all_tables <- read_aofm() length(all_tables)  tb_all <- read_aofm("tb") names(tb_all)

title: readAOFM
text: search_aofm() searches supported table catalog returns matching tables together ready--run read_aofm() calls. want data immediately, set read = TRUE: downstream analysis charting, add preferred tidyverse packages calling script:
code: search_aofm("tb issuance") search_aofm("issuance") search_aofm("treasury bond") search_aofm("inflation") tb_issuance <- search_aofm("tb issuance", read = TRUE) all_issuance <- search_aofm("issuance", read = TRUE) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(readAOFM)  read_aofm("tb", "issuance") %>%   filter(name == "coverage_ratio") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = date_held, y = value)) +   geom_point() +   theme_minimal()

title: readAOFM
text: download_aofm_xlsx() still available want raw workbooks saved locally, read_aofm() preferred interface normal use.

title: readAOFM
text: AOFM website may change ways require updates package. package affiliated endorsed AOFM. data provided terms set AOFM website.

title: Download xlsx.xls files from AOFM DataHub — download_aofm_xlsx
code: if (FALSE) download_aofm_xlsx("tb", "issuance") # \dontrun{} # downloads AOFM Treasury Bond Issuance data to data/tb_issuance.xlsx if (FALSE) download_aofm_xlsx("tb") # \dontrun{} # downloads all Treasury Bond data from the AOFM Datahub incl # issunace, syndication details, position data etc to data/ if (FALSE) download_aofm_xlsx() # \dontrun{} # downloads all data from the AOFM Datahub to data/

title: Reads in and cleans any data from the AOFM, returns cleaned in a long format — read_aofm
text: security object want data , options include: tb, tib, tn, slf, summary, aggregate, ownership, retail, termpremium. Optional; omitted, supported tables returned. type specific type data want, optional inputs resolve one supported tables: dealt, settlement, issuance, syndication, buyback, turnover, csv TRUE, also writes parsed output CSV file via underlying reader

title: Reads in and cleans the
code: if (FALSE)  read_eom("tb_position_dealt") # \dontrun{} # downloads Treasury Bond EOM data on a dealt basis from the AOFM # returns cleaned in a long format # data is returned as a list with data frame elements for # FaceValue, MarketValue, Delta, Duration and Tenor if (FALSE) read_eom("tb_position_settlement") # \dontrun{} # downloads Treasury Bond EOM data on a settlement basis from the # AOFM, returns cleaned in a long format as a list with elements for each of # FaceValue, MarketValue, Delta, Duration and Tenor

Search the supported AOFM table catalog — search_aofm
text: query Search string used match supported AOFM tables read TRUE, reads matched tables via read_aofm() instead returning catalog rows csv TRUE read = TRUE, also writes matched outputs CSV via read_aofm()

title: Search the supported AOFM table catalog — search_aofm
text: dataframe supported table matches, result read_aofm() read = TRUE

title: Search the supported AOFM table catalog — search_aofm
code: search_aofm("tb issuance") #>   security     type          id             reader                   read_call #> 1       tb issuance tb_issuance read_transactional read_aofm("tb", "issuance") search_aofm("issuance") #>   security     type           id             reader #> 1       tb issuance  tb_issuance read_transactional #> 2      tib issuance tib_issuance read_transactional #> 3       tn issuance  tn_issuance read_transactional #>                      read_call #> 1  read_aofm("tb", "issuance") #> 2 read_aofm("tib", "issuance") #> 3  read_aofm("tn", "issuance") search_aofm("treasury bond") #>   security        type                     id             reader #> 1       tb     buyback             tb_buyback read_transactional #> 2       tb       dealt      tb_position_dealt           read_eom #> 3       tb    issuance            tb_issuance read_transactional #> 4       tb  settlement tb_position_settlement           read_eom #> 5       tb syndication         tb_syndication   read_syndication #> 6       tb    turnover            tb_turnover     read_secondary #>                        read_call #> 1     read_aofm("tb", "buyback") #> 2       read_aofm("tb", "dealt") #> 3    read_aofm("tb", "issuance") #> 4  read_aofm("tb", "settlement") #> 5 read_aofm("tb", "syndication") #> 6    read_aofm("tb", "turnover") search_aofm("inflation") #>   security        type                      id             reader #> 1      tib     buyback             tib_buyback read_transactional #> 2      tib       dealt      tib_position_dealt           read_eom #> 3      tib    issuance            tib_issuance read_transactional #> 4      tib  settlement tib_position_settlement           read_eom #> 5      tib syndication         tib_syndication   read_syndication #> 6      tib    turnover            tib_turnover     read_secondary #>                         read_call #> 1     read_aofm("tib", "buyback") #> 2       read_aofm("tib", "dealt") #> 3    read_aofm("tib", "issuance") #> 4  read_aofm("tib", "settlement") #> 5 read_aofm("tib", "syndication") #> 6    read_aofm("tib", "turnover") if (FALSE) search_aofm("tb issuance", read = TRUE) # \dontrun{}
